source: NFPA 10 + NFPA 13 + NFPA 30 + OSHA 29 CFR 1910.157 + IBC 2021 Chapter 9
topic: Industrial fire safety — extinguisher placement, sprinkler clearances, hazard separation zones

facts.context: NFPA 10 — max 75 ft travel distance to a Class A extinguisher (ordinary combustibles)
NFPA 10 — max 50 ft travel distance to a Class B extinguisher (flammable liquids)
OSHA 1910.157(c)(6) — extinguishers over 18 kg mounted no higher than 1.07m; lighter units max 60 in (1.524m)
OSHA 1910.157 — 36 in minimum clear access maintained to all fire extinguisher locations
NFPA 13 — minimum 18 in clear below sprinkler deflector — storage and equipment must not encroach
NFPA 13 — minimum 12 in lateral clear zone around each sprinkler head
NFPA 13 — max 15 ft between sprinkler heads in ordinary hazard industrial occupancy
NFPA 13 — max 12 ft between heads in extra hazard group 2 (flammable liquid areas)
NFPA 30 — flammable liquid storage must be separated from ignition sources by min 25 ft
Minimum clear access zone around flammable storage cabinets — front and sides
IBC 414 — hazardous material rooms must have 10 ft separation from exit routes
NFPA 51B — 35 ft separation between welding operations and combustible materials
Fire doors must have 36 in clear zone on both sides — no storage or equipment in swing path
Minimum 36 in clear zone in front of fire hose cabinets — same as extinguishers
ANSI Z358.1 — 48 in clear zone around emergency shower/eyewash stations — must be reachable within 10 seconds
ANSI Z358.1 — emergency shower must be within 100 ft (10 second walk) from hazardous chemical exposure areas
NFPA 72 — smoke detectors spaced no more than 30 ft apart in industrial ceiling configuration
Electrical rooms and switchgear must maintain 6 ft separation from flammable storage areas
